perturbation.original_text: This Agreement ("Agreement") is made effective March 22, 2018, by and between Freeze Tag Inc., a Delaware Corporation ("Company"), with its principal place of business located at 1720 Bray Central Drive, McKinney, TX 75069 and the American Diabetes Association, Inc. ("Association"), an Ohio not-for profit corporation, with its principal place of business located at 2451 Crystal Drive, Suite 900, Arlington, VA 22202.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement ("Agreement") is made effective March 22, 2018, by and between Freeze Tag Inc., a Delaware Corporation ("Company"), with its principal place of business located at 1720 Bray Central Drive, McKinney, TX 75069 and the American Diabetes Association, Inc. ("Association"), an Ohio for-profit corporation, with its principal place of business located at 2451 Crystal Drive, Suite 900, Arlington, VA 22202.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'not-for profit corporation' to 'for-profit corporation' directly contradicts Section 2, which states 'Company agrees not to knowingly take any actions that would jeopardize the tax-exempt status of Association under section 501(c)(3) of the Code.' A for-profit association would not have tax-exempt status, creating an immediate conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company agrees not to knowingly take any actions that would jeopardize the tax-exempt status of Association under section 501(c)(3) of the Code.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'not-for profit corporation' to 'for-profit corporation' directly contradicts the clause 'Company agrees not to knowingly take any actions that would jeopardize the tax-exempt status of Association under section 501(c)(3) of the Code.' A for-profit corporation is not eligible for 501(c)(3) status. This creates a direct conflict and introduces uncertainty about the Company's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 14. Termination: Before expiration of the Term, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon: (i) any material breach of the Agreement by the other Party, if such breach is not remedied to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party within ten (10) business days after written notice; (ii) ten (10) business days written notice to the other Party whenever the notifying Party in its sole discretion determines that the continuation of the Agreement will damage its reputation or good will; or (iii) written notice in the event one Party (a) becomes or is declared insolvent or bankrupt or is subject to the appointment of a trustee or receiver or any equivalent thereof, (b) is the subject of any proceeding related to its liquidation or insolvency (whether voluntary or involuntary) which is not dismissed within ninety (90) days, (c) makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or (d) is subject to any sale, lease or other transfer of all or substantially all of its assets to any entity; or (e) is subject to a change of control (whether by merger, stock transfer or otherwise), except in the case of an initial public offering.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Termination: Before expiration of the Term, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon: (i) any material breach of the Agreement by the other Party, if such breach is not remedied to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party within one-hundred and eighty (180) business days after written notice; (ii) ten (10) business days written notice to the other Party whenever the notifying Party in its sole discretion determines that the continuation of the Agreement will damage its reputation or good will; or (iii) written notice in the event one Party (a) becomes or is declared insolvent or bankrupt or is subject to the appointment of a trustee or receiver or any equivalent thereof, (b) is the subject of any proceeding related to its liquidation or insolvency (whether voluntary or involuntary) which is not dismissed within ninety (90) days, (c) makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or (d) is subject to any sale, lease or other transfer of all or substantially all of its assets to any entity; or (e) is subject to a change of control (whether by merger, stock transfer or otherwise), except in the case of an initial public offering. The American Diabetes Association, Inc. (“Association”), may also terminate this agreement for any reason with (10) days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the cure period for a material breach from 'ten (10) business days' to 'one-hundred and eighty (180) business days' creates a contradiction, this is combined with adding the sentence: 'The American Diabetes Association, Inc. (“Association”), may also terminate this agreement for any reason with (10) days written notice.', the ADA now has the option to terminate on short notice for any reason regardless of whether or not freeze tag is working to amend the material breach for a period up to 'one-hundred and eighty (180) business days'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Termination: Before expiration of the Term, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon: (i) any material breach of the Agreement by the other Party, if such breach is not remedied to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party within one-hundred and eighty (180) business days after written notice; (ii) ten (10) business days written notice to the other Party whenever the notifying Party in its sole discretion determines that the continuation of the Agreement will damage its reputation or good will; or (iii) written notice in the event one Party (a) becomes or is declared insolvent or bankrupt or is subject to the appointment of a trustee or receiver or any equivalent thereof, (b) is the subject of any proceeding related to its liquidation or insolvency (whether voluntary or involuntary) which is not dismissed within ninety (90) days, (c) makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or (d) is subject to any sale, lease or other transfer of all or substantially all of its assets to any entity; or (e) is subject to a change of control (whether by merger, stock transfer or otherwise), except in the case of an initial public offering. The American Diabetes Association, Inc. (“Association”), may also terminate this agreement for any reason with (10) days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original clause states that either party can terminate for a material breach if not remedied within 10 business days. The changed text extends the cure period to 180 days but simultaneously allows the ADA to terminate for *any* reason with only 10 days' notice. This directly contradicts the extended cure period, as the ADA could terminate even if the breach is being actively remedied within the 180-day window, rendering that period meaningless. This inconsistency creates uncertainty and conflicts regarding the obligations of the parties with respect to breach and termination.

perturbation.original_text: 19. Confidentiality. The provisions of this Agreement shall be maintained by the Parties as confidential during the Term and thereafter. In addition, any and all aspects of Company's business, including without limitation all non-public information or trade secrets directly or indirectly related thereto, that Association becomes exposed to during the Term, and extensions or renewals, of this Agreement shall be maintained as confidential, and shall not be further disclosed by Association, or used by Association for any purpose other than performing hereunder during the Term or thereafter. Company shall at all times retain full ownership in and to all information respecting its business, and shall be the sole and exclusive owner of all materials created by or for the Company hereunder, with the exception of the Association Marks.
perturbation.changed_text: 19. Confidentiality. The provisions of this Agreement shall be maintained by the Parties as confidential during the Term and thereafter, except for instances where disclosure is mandated by law or regulatory requirements. In addition, any and all aspects of Company's business, including without limitation all non-public information or trade secrets directly or indirectly related thereto, that Association becomes exposed to during the Term, and extensions or renewals, of this Agreement shall be maintained as confidential, and shall not be further disclosed by Association, or used by Association for any purpose other than performing hereunder during the Term or thereafter. Company shall at all times retain full ownership in and to all information respecting its business, and shall be the sole and exclusive owner of all materials created by or for the Company hereunder, with the exception of the Association Marks. The Association shall have the right to disclose confidential information to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors who have a need to know such information for the purpose of carrying out the Association's obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'except for instances where disclosure is mandated by law or regulatory requirements', the obligation for confidentiality is lessened, but adding the sentence: 'The Association shall have the right to disclose confidential information to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors who have a need to know such information for the purpose of carrying out the Association's obligations under this Agreement.' completely nullifies it. With the exception now in place, this is not only referring to legal reasons, but it also grants the association full discretion to disclose confidential information to any agent or contractor. This creates a contradiction with the initial confidentiality agreement, and makes the clause less clear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 19. Confidentiality. The provisions of this Agreement shall be maintained by the Parties as confidential during the Term and thereafter, except for instances where disclosure is mandated by law or regulatory requirements. In addition, any and all aspects of Company's business, including without limitation all non-public information or trade secrets directly or indirectly related thereto, that Association becomes exposed to during the Term, and extensions or renewals, of this Agreement shall be maintained as confidential, and shall not be further disclosed by Association, or used by Association for any purpose other than performing hereunder during the Term or thereafter. Company shall at all times retain full ownership in and to all information respecting its business, and shall be the sole and exclusive owner of all materials created by or for the Company hereunder, with the exception of the Association Marks. The Association shall have the right to disclose confidential information to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors who have a need to know such information for the purpose of carrying out the Association's obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates confidentiality 'during the Term and thereafter.' The changed text adds an exception for disclosures mandated by law. However, it further adds that 'The Association shall have the right to disclose confidential information to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors who have a need to know such information for the purpose of carrying out the Association's obligations under this Agreement.' This second addition significantly broadens the exceptions to confidentiality, potentially nullifying the initial confidentiality obligation. The original intent of confidentiality is contradicted by granting the Association broad discretion to disclose information, creating a direct conflict. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
